title: rosa canina subsp. coriifolia
intro: Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia, commonly known as the Dog Rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina species, characterized by its thorny stems and fragrant, pink to white flowers. The health benefits of Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of this plant include its use as a diuretic, astringent, and anti-inflammatory agent, as well as its potential to support wound healing and protect against certain infections. The bioactive constituents of Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid, among others, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia has many benefits, such as being a rich source of vitamin C and antioxidants, which help to boost the immune system and protect against cell damage, ultimately reducing the risk of chronic diseases like cancer, heart disease, and neurodegenerative disorders. This plant also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, reduce swelling and pain, and even help to manage menstrual cramps and other digestive issues. Additionally, the rose hips from this plant are high in fiber, which can aid in digestion and promote healthy bowel movements, supporting the overall well-being of an individual and improving their quality of life. By incorporating Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia into one's diet or using its extracts topically, individuals can experience a range of health benefits that can improve their physical and mental well-being, leading to a happier and more fulfilling life.
actions: Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit wound healing, anti-aging, and anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable resource for improving skin health and reducing the risk of certain diseases. By using Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia in skincare products, individuals can enjoy improved skin elasticity, reduced appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, and enhanced protection against environmental stressors, ultimately leading to a more youthful and radiant complexion. Additionally, the plant's antiseptic and antibacterial properties make it an effective natural remedy for treating minor cuts and scrapes, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
constituents: Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer. The plant also contains phenolic acids like gallic acid and chlorogenic acid, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, providing relief from infections and colds. Additionally, Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia is rich in essential oils, including geraniol, linalool, and limonene, which have been used to treat anxiety and insomnia, promoting better sleep quality and overall well-being. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia, individuals can experience improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced mental clarity, leading to a more balanced and fulfilling life.
parts: Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia has many medicinal parts, such as its fragrant flowers, which are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, including quercetin and kaempferol, that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of this plant contain tannins, including gallic acid and epigallocatechin gallate, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. The seeds of Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia are an excellent source of vitamin C and contain carotenoids and xanthophylls, such as beta-carotene and lutein, which contribute to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the plant's hips or fruit, which are rich in ascorbic acid, anthocyanins, and other polyphenols, have been used in traditional medicine to boost the immune system and treat various health conditions.
preparations: Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia has many herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used for medicinal purposes. Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia tea is used to treat digestive issues and is also employed as a mild diuretic, while its tincture is used to treat menstrual cramps and as a natural remedy for anxiety. The salves prepared from Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia are used topically to treat wounds, cuts, and skin irritations, utilizing its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Infusions of Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia are also used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and are valued for their expectorant properties.
side_effects: Rosa canina subsp. coriifolia can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interact with certain medications. Taking large doses of the plant's extracts can lead to gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps. Some individuals may also experience skin irritation, hives, and itchy rashes after coming into contact with the plant's sap or using its oil topically. In rare cases, consuming large amounts of the plant's fruit or extracts can cause kidney and liver damage due to its high content of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds.
